title: Fix tab-bar truncation for long localized tab labels
reframed: Original scope (UI tweak with lineLimit/truncationMode/minimumScaleFactor) was deferred after prior screenshot evidence showed iOS already auto-shrinks the longer tab labels rather than truncating at iPhone 17 portrait default Dynamic Type. New scope, approved by user via AskUserQuestion: extend LocalizationAccessibilityRegression with maintained coverage at AccessibilityXL for the 7 newly-flagged locales (fr, ja, nl, ru, tr, uk, ar). de + en already covered.
summary: Added 7 new XCUITest methods to LocalizationAccessibilityRegression. Each launches a fresh-day seeded Owlory in the target locale with UICTContentSizeCategoryAccessibilityXL and asserts the Today dashboard header is reachable plus the root tab bar exposes 5 hittable buttons. All 11 accessibility tests pass on iPhone 17 / iOS 26.5 simulator in 66.8s. The full DOMAIN=localization suite (15 tests: 11 accessibility + 4 layout) passes in 91.8s. No app source or translation changes. Closed 8 tab-truncation findings via maintained-coverage state (HIG-AR-003 + HIG-DE-002 + HIG-FR-001 + HIG-JA-001 + HIG-NL-001 + HIG-RU-001 + HIG-TR-001 + HIG-UK-001).
scope_deviation: owlory_xcode/OwloryUITests/ is not strictly under owlory_xcode/Owlory/ in this slice's allowed_paths, but the reframed scope is XCUITest expansion and matches the required_validations (make ui-regression DOMAIN=localization). The original UI tweak scope (still under owlory_xcode/Owlory/) was deferred because evidence did not justify it. Recorded honestly.
not_claimed: smaller iPhone width verification (iPhone SE etc.)
VoiceOver listening verification under non-English locales
screenshot evidence at AccessibilityXL (the regression asserts behavior, doesn't preserve a screenshot)
residual_risk: AccessibilityXL regressions assert the shell settles and tabs are hittable but do not measure label readability at the auto-shrunk size. The text may render legibly but smaller for the longer-label tabs; only a VoiceOver / vision-test pass would catch readability concerns.
Smaller iPhone widths (iPhone SE physical or simulator) are not covered. The current regression targets iPhone 17 only.
The maintained regression is locale-specific; if a new locale is added later with an even longer tab label, that locale must be added explicitly to LocalizationAccessibilityRegression.
